what_it_does: Defines a derived chrome surface-mode rule that maps imagery/render grounds to product chrome and paper/surface-token grounds to document chrome, with mode-specific controls, cards, hotspots, and per-surface resolution notes.
observations: Meta identifies version modes-3 and frames the file as a CHROME-MODE face with two presets keyed to ground.
The rule says surface_mode is derived from ground: imagery/render becomes product, paper/surface tokens becomes document.
Document mode defines outlined light pill controls and tinted plate cards using surface, accent, radius, and shadow tokens.
Product mode defines dark glass controls, frosted white cards, and small circular render hotspots.
Resolution is per surface with nearest-scope-wins behavior and nested grounds.
The file includes free-text provenance, factory mapping, retro note, and product verification evidence.
standouts: Chrome mode is explicitly derived rather than chosen.
The document/product split is described as a boundary between documents about the product and the product's own surfaces.
Nested grounds allow a product-chrome frame inside a paper page.
Product verification records tour viewer, info surfaces, editor, analytics, captures ledger, and white-label notes.
conventions: Uses JSON as a design-token and design-law ledger with prose-heavy string fields.
Uses brace-delimited token references such as {color.surface.line} and {chrome.glass}.
Separates modes into document and product branches with controls and cards sections.
Keeps evidence and provenance as descriptive strings rather than structured source records.
concerns: Several fields combine rule, rationale, examples, and evidence in long prose strings, which may be hard to parse mechanically.
Provenance and evidence are not structured into source, date, page, and claim fields.
factory_mapping references an external mechanism without a local identifier or schema link.
notes: This file records the rule that chrome styling is resolved from each surface's ground, with document and product presets.
questions: What exact values or detector decide whether a ground is imagery/render or paper/surface tokens?
What schema, if any, consumes factory_mapping, provenance, and product_verified?
Are hotspots intentionally product-only, or just only defined there in this file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it defines a derived two-mode chrome dial and documents how it resolves per surface.
intention_signals: It seems intended to prevent manual or mismatched chrome choices by making visual treatment follow the local ground type, including nested product/document surfaces.
novelty: A chrome preset is treated as a derived design law with nested ground resolution rather than as a manually selected theme.
contribution: Contributes a surface-ground-to-chrome mapping, document and product chrome definitions, nested resolution behavior, hotspot treatment, and verification notes that a fused system could retain as contextual styling logic.
summary_for_embedding: This is a JSON design ledger defining derived document and product chrome modes based on surface ground, with tokenized controls, cards, hotspots, resolution behavior, and verification notes.
intention_for_embedding: It was for encoding chrome styling as a contextual rule that separates document surfaces from product surfaces and resolves locally across nested grounds.
